description:
You are a cloud architect who has just finished preparing the development landing zone for a micro-services migration.  
To signal that the dev environment is officially “migration-ready”, create a **single text file** whose exact contents will be checked by an automated pipeline.

Requirements for the file:
1. Path: **/home/user/dev_env_ready.txt**  
2. Permissions: it only needs to be readable by the current user (default `umask` settings are fine).  
3. Contents: the file must contain **exactly one ASCII line** consisting solely of the 17 uppercase characters  
```
READY_FOR_MIGRATION
```  
   • No leading or trailing spaces.  
   • No extra blank lines before or after.  
   • No newline character at the end of the file (file size must be 17 bytes).  

Task:
• Use a single terminal command of your choice to create this file with the correct contents at the specified location.  
• When you are done, you may run any harmless read-only commands (for example, `cat`, `wc -c`, or `stat`) to show that the file exists and is exactly 17 bytes long, but the creation itself must be accomplished with **one compound shell invocation**.

The CI test will fail if:
• The path or filename is different.  
• The file contains additional whitespace, newlines, or characters.  
• The file size is not exactly 17 bytes.

This is the only deliverable—no additional files or directories should be created.
truth:
Expected artifact for verification:  
• File: /home/user/dev_env_ready.txt  
• Size: 17 bytes  
• Byte-for-byte contents (hex dump):  
  52 45 41 44 59 5F 46 4F 52 5F 4D 49 47 52 41 54 49 4F 4E  
  (ASCII text: “READY_FOR_MIGRATION” with **no terminating newline**)

The automated test will confirm that:
1. `test -f /home/user/dev_env_ready.txt` returns exit status 0.  
2. `wc -c < /home/user/dev_env_ready.txt` outputs `17`.  
3. `xxd -p /home/user/dev_env_ready.txt` yields `52454144595f464f525f4d4947524154494f4e`.  
4. No extra files matching `/home/user/dev_env_ready.*` exist.
name: task_000000_b0efede8
